reconstruction_method: Neo-Hittite cuneiform syllabic spelling + plene vowel writing (V/VV) + comparative Anatolian (Luwian/Palaic) + Akkadian loanword evidence + PIE laryngeal confirmation via ḫ
scholarly_consensus: Melchert/Kimball standard: four vowels /a e i u/ with phonemic length; fortis/lenis (geminate vs single) stop contrast dominant over voicing; ḫ as uvular/velar fricative; no distinct voiced stops in standard model; cuneiform maps syllables reliably in Neo-Hittite orthography.
confidence_overview: medium (high on consonant inventory, ḫ, and vowel length; medium on unstressed vowel qualities, exact fortis articulation, and stress placement)
laryngeal_notes: Hittite ḫ (cuneiform ḫa/ḫi etc.) preserves PIE laryngeals *h2/*h3 as a guttural fricative [x~χ] (uvular to velar); colors adjacent vowels (e.g. *h2 → a-coloring); key empirical proof of the laryngeal theory unavailable in Greek, Latin, or Sanskrit.

vowels: /a e i u/ with phonemic length /aː eː iː uː/ (plene writing and comparative evidence); unstressed qualities partly inferred
consonants: Stops: p pp t tt k kk (fortis/lenis); Fricatives: s š ḫ [x~χ]; Nasals: m n; Liquids: l r; Glides: y w; no distinct b d g in standard Melchert model
laryngeals: ḫ = [x~χ] (PIE *h2/*h3 reflex); possible allophonic variation uvular vs velar
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: Fortis/lenis stop contrast; preserved laryngeals; vowel reduction in unstressed syllables; Indo-European's oldest attested branch

ipa_reconstructed.sample_text: namma antuhsas andan esdu (ritual formula: 'let mankind remain inside')
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Consonants and ḫ secure; vowel qualities in antuhsas/esdu reconstructed from plene spellings; stress inferred from Anatolian comparative patterns
ipa_reconstructed.alternatives: Sturtevant voiced/voiceless model; penultimate stress hypothesis; ḫ as [ħ] in older literature

stress_rules: Likely initial or penultimate stress (Anatolian comparative evidence); exact pattern disputed — conservative default: stress first full syllable of content word

prosody.rhythm: Syllable-timed with fortis geminates lengthening syllables
prosody.pacing: Formulaic ritual/legal diction; deliberate enunciation in cult texts
prosody.notes: Cuneiform does not mark stress; pacing inferred from parallel Luwian and meter in hymns

grok_imagine_guidance: Commit to: guttural ḫ as back-of-throat fricative (Scottish 'loch' / German 'Bach', not English h); fortis geminates held longer (pp/tt/kk); pure vowels a/e/i/u; no English voiced b/d/g defaults. Approximate: š as [ʃ]; long vowels from double VV spellings sustained ~1.5×. Avoid: treating ḫ as silent or as kh; Slavic-style soft consonants; Modern Turkish vowel harmony. Flag: ḫ has no English equivalent — describe as uvular scrape; geminate stops need audible hold.
tutoring_hooks: Hittite is the OLDEST attested Indo-European language (~1650 BCE) — older than Greek or Sanskrit texts.
Hittite ḫ preserved PIE laryngeals that vanished everywhere else — proof of the laryngeal theory!
Cuneiform looks like bird tracks but spells syllables: ḫa = ḫ+a, not a logogram.
Fortis/lenis (double vs single consonants) matters more than voiced/voiceless in standard reconstruction.
The Hittite Laws open with a formula every student of ancient law recognizes — in a language dead 3,000 years.

sources.author: H. Craig Melchert
sources.title: Hittite Historical Phonology (in The Hittite Language)

sources.author: Alwin Kloekhorst
sources.title: Etymological Dictionary of the Hittite Inherited Lexicon
